rulings.Case Title: NIC INSURANCE COMPANY VS. LIBERTY SURPLUS INSURANCE CO., A NEW YORK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Liberty Surplus Insurance Co'S Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Three
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 5, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF TOPA INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Liberty Surplus Insurance Co's Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Three. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Defendant's Further Responses to Requests for Production of Documents (Set Three) is DENIED. The Court finds that (1) Defendant Liberty Surplus Insurance Co. has reasonably complied with Request for Production #13; and (2) production of the document requested in Request for Production #15 is not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, October 5, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANTS REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, TODD MCGREGOR, JAMES JACOBS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Off calendar. Case settled. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LISA DE LA SOL VS. THE DOCUMENT COMPANY INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, October 5, 2012, Line 18, DEFENDANT XEROX CORPORATION, A NEW YORK CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. Xerox satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any evidence creating a triable issue whether Xerox is vicariously liable for the conduct of Mr. Coker that caused plaintiff's injuries. The Business Relationship Agreement and Authorized Sales Agent Schedule only show an agency relationship between the defendants for the sales of certain Xerox products. The Authorized Sales Agent Schedule does not demonstrate the right to control the manner and means of repairing Xerox products necessary to establish an agency relationship during the repair or servicing of Xerox products. It is undisputed at the time of the accident Mr. Coker was driving home after servicing a Xerox machine. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LAUREL TIMMS et al VS. YING Y. WANG AKA THERESA WANG, TRUSTEE OF THE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Quashing Deposition Subpoenas, Barring Or Limiting Depositions, And Awarding Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 5, 2012, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS LAUREL TIMMS and MATT MCNALLY'S Motion For An Order Quashing Deposition Subpoenas, Barring Or Limiting Depositions, And Awarding Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' Motion for an Order Quashing Deposition Subpoenas, Barring or Limiting Depositions, and Awarding Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED IN PART. The deposition subpoenas at issue for AMARENA RESTAURANT, COCOA DEVELOPMENT RETAIL, LLC, JMA GHIRARDELLI INVESTORS LLC, COOP KITCHENS, CORNERSTONE, ITK, AND MEDITERREANEO shall be limited as follows: (1) The documents and things to be produced shall be limited to "Any and all documents relating to the rental and/or use of the deponent's kitchen facility by Laurel's Catering for the time period of March 15, 2008 through December 31, 2010;" and (2) The matters upon which the witness is to be examined shall be limited to "The rental and/or use of the deponent's kitchen facility by Laurel's Catering for the time period of March 15, 2008 through December 31, 2010." Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DALE C. WEBB VS. LORRAINE O. LEGG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS GENERAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, October 5, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 2, 3 and 4 and denied as to issues 1, 5 and 6. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not submit any evidence creating a triable issue as to the three fraud claims. There are triable issues whether defendant breached any fiduciary duty it owed to plaitniff, acted negligently toward plaintiff, the July 13 letter imposes any contract obligations on defendant in favor of plaintiff, and defendant breached any such contract obligations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WILLIAMS VS ODELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Request For Osc And Affidavit For Contempt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, October 5, 2012, Line 17, PLAINTIFF MARQUIS WILLIAMS' Motion For Request For Osc And Affidavit For Contempt. United Labor Bank must pay the full principal and accrued interest to Mr. Williams within two weeks. ULB acknowledges that it had at one time had possession of Mr. Williams' funds. It was obligated by the 1990 order not to transfer those funds to the State for escheat, but apparently did so. While doing so was probably a mistake, the loss due to its mistake should be borne by ULB, not Mr. Williams. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
